sentence: الْأَرْض وَاسْعَة وَالْقَمَر طَالِع فَوْق الْجِبَال.
raw: الأرض واسعة والقمر طالع فوق الجبال.
ipa: alˈʔarɮˤ ˈwaːsʕa ˈwalqamar ˈtˤɑːliʕ ˈfawq aldʒiˈbaːl
gloss_en: The land is wide and the moon is up over the mountains.
notes: ض→[ɮˤ] voiced lateral emphatic in الأرض (watson2011 p.428; asiri2009); ق retained [q] الْقَمَر→[alqamar] southwestern retention (asiri2009); diphthong فَوْق

sentence: وِش تِبْغَى تَاكُل الظُّهْر يَا وَلَدِي؟
raw: وش تبغى تاكل الظهر يا ولدي؟
ipa: ˈwiʃ ˈtibɣaː ˈtaːkul aɬˤˈɬˤuhr ˈjaː ˈwaladiː
gloss_en: What do you want to eat at noon, my son?
notes: ظ→[ɬˤ] voiceless lateral emphatic الظُّهْر→[aɬˤɬˤuhr] (watson2011 p.428); *ḍ/*ẓ kept distinct from the ض of الأرض

sentence: مَا عِنْدِي ضَيْم مِنْك، بَس تَعَال بَعْدَين.
raw: ما عندي ضيم منك، بس تعال بعدين.
ipa: ˈmaː ˈʕindiː ˈɮˤajm ˈmink ˈbas taˈʕaːl baʕˈdajn
gloss_en: I have no grievance against you, just come later.
notes: ضَيْم→[ɮˤajm] *ḍ voiced lateral (watson2011 p.428, cf. al-Rubūʿah ḍlaym 'pain'); pharyngeal ع; diphthong بَعْدَين

sentence: الظِّل بَارِد تَحْت هَذِي الشَّجَرَة الْكَبِيرَة.
raw: الظل بارد تحت هذي الشجرة الكبيرة.
ipa: ˈaɬˤɬˤil ˈbaːrid ˈtaħt ˈhaðiː ʃˈʃadʒara lkaˈbiːra
gloss_en: The shade is cool under this big tree.
notes: ظ→[ɬˤ] الظِّل→[aɬˤɬˤil] (watson2011 p.428; alazraqi2010); sun assim الشَّجَرَة; interdental ذ retained هَذِي

sentence: خُذ ثَلَاث حَبَّات ضَان وَاعْطِنِي وَاحِدَة.
raw: خذ ثلاث حبات ضان واعطني واحدة.
ipa: ˈxuð θaˈlaːθ ħabˈbaːt ˈɮˤaːn ˈwaːʕtˤiniː ˈwaːħida
gloss_en: Take three head of sheep and give me one.
notes: imperatives xuð/aʕtˤ; ث retained [θ] (asiri2009); ضَان→[ɮˤaːn] *ḍ voiced lateral emphatic (watson2011 p.428); geminate حَبَّات

sentence: هَذَا الرِّجَّال يِضْرِب الطَّبْل فِي الْعُرْس.
raw: هذا الرجال يضرب الطبل في العرس.
ipa: ˈhaðaː rridʒˈdʒaːl ˈjiɮˤrib ɑtˤˈtˤɑbl ˈfiː lˈʕurs
gloss_en: This man beats the drum at the wedding.
notes: يِضْرِب→[jiɮˤrib] *ḍ voiced lateral (watson2011 p.428); emphatic ط backing; geminate رِّجَّال; pharyngeal ع

sentence: السَّيْل فَاض بَعْد الْمَطَر وَمَا ظَلّ شَيّ.
raw: السيل فاض بعد المطر وما ظل شي.
ipa: asˈsajl ˈfaːɮˤ ˈbaʕd ˈalmɑtˤɑr ˈwamaː ˈɬˤall ˈʃajj
gloss_en: The flood overflowed after the rain and nothing remained.
notes: فَاض→[faːɮˤ] *ḍ voiced lateral emphatic — the paper's attested near-minimal-pair member fāḍ 'it overfilled' (watson2011 p.428); ظَلّ→[ɬˤall] *ẓ voiceless lateral emphatic kept distinct (watson2011 p.428; alazraqi2010); sayl/wādī flood register fits the Tihāmah terrain (asiri2009); sun assim السَّيْل

sentence: بِكَم كِيلُو الْعَسَل؟ بِعِشْرِين رِيَال.
raw: بكم كيلو العسل؟ بعشرين ريال.
ipa: ˈbikam ˈkiːluː ˈalʕasal biʕiʃˈriːn riˈjaːl
gloss_en: How much is the kilo of honey? Twenty riyals.
notes: عَسَل honey is the pan-peninsular staple of the ʿAsīr/Tihāmah economy (asiri2009); بِكَم price interrogative and عِشْرِين numeral are conservative pan-peninsular

sentence: الْمَوْعِد بُكْرَة بَعْد صَلَاة الْعَصْر.
raw: الموعد بكرة بعد صلاة العصر.
ipa: alˈmawʕid ˈbukra ˈbaʕd ˈsˤɑlaː lˈʕɑsˤr
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow after the afternoon prayer.
notes: بُكْرَة 'tomorrow' and prayer-time reckoning are pan-peninsular (asiri2009); صَلَاة الْعَصْر construct with tā-marbūṭa liaison

sentence: أَبُوي يَسْكُن فِي الْقَرْيَة فَوْق الْجَبَل.
raw: أبوي يسكن في القرية فوق الجبل.
ipa: ʔaˈbuːj ˈjaskun ˈfiː lˈqarja ˈfawq ˈaldʒabal
gloss_en: My father lives in the village up the mountain.
notes: قَرْيَة→[qarja] qaf retained [q], the southwestern sedentary reflex, NOT Najdi [ɡ] (asiri2009; watson2011); أَبُوي 'my father' conservative peninsular; highland residence fits the ʿAsīr terrain; diphthong فَوْق

sentence: رُوح عَلَى طُول وَبَعْدَين لِفّ يَمِين عِنْد الْمَسْجِد.
raw: روح على طول وبعدين لف يمين عند المسجد.
ipa: ˈruːħ ˈʕalaː ˈtˤuːl wabaʕˈdajn ˈliff jaˈmiːn ˈʕind alˈmasdʒid
gloss_en: Go straight then turn right at the mosque.
notes: imperatives رُوح/لِفّ and عَلَى طُول 'straight on' are conservative pan-peninsular directives (asiri2009); geminate لِفّ; diphthong بَعْدَين

sentence: الْعَشَا أَمْس كَان طَيِّب وَاجِد.
raw: العشا أمس كان طيب واجد.
ipa: ˈalʕaʃaː ˈʔams ˈkaːn ˈtˤɑjjib ˈwaːdʒid
gloss_en: The dinner yesterday was very tasty.
notes: وَاجِد 'very/much' is the southwestern/south-Arabian intensifier (watson2002; asiri2009), preferred here over the northern كَثِير; geminate طَيِّب

sentence: وِين أَخُوي؟ مَا هُو فِي الْبَيْت.
raw: وين أخوي؟ ما هو في البيت.
ipa: ˈwiːn ʔaˈxuːj ˈmaː ˈhuː ˈfiː lˈbajt
gloss_en: Where is my brother? He is not at home.
notes: wīn 'where' spelled with kasra to yield the long monophthong (avoids the وَين word-initial seam); mā-hū predicate negation; diphthong retained الْبَيْت→[bajt]; conservative southwestern sedentary phonology per (asiri2009)

sentence: كَم وَلَد عِنْدَك يَا خَالِد؟
raw: كم ولد عندك يا خالد؟
ipa: ˈkam ˈwalad ˈʕindak ˈjaː ˈxaːlid
gloss_en: How many children do you have, Khalid?
notes: كَم quantity interrogative + وَلَد count noun; conservative pan-peninsular; conservative southwestern sedentary phonology per (asiri2009)

sentence: إِذَا ضِعْت الطَّرِيق اسْأَل أَهْل الْقَرْيَة.
raw: إذا ضعت الطريق اسأل أهل القرية.
ipa: ˈʔiðaː ˈɮˤiʕt ɑtˤtˤɑˈriːq ˈʔsʔal ˈʔahl alˈqarja
gloss_en: If you lose the way, ask the people of the village.
notes: ضِعْت→[ɮˤiʕt] *ḍ voiced lateral emphatic (watson2011 p.428; alazraqi2010); إِذَا conditional + imperative اسْأَل; قَرْيَة qaf retained [q] (asiri2009); sun assim الطَّرِيق

sentence: رُحْت السُّوق، اشْتَرَيْت خُضَار وَلَحَم.
raw: رحت السوق، اشتريت خضار ولحم.
ipa: ˈruħt asˈsuːq ʔʃtaˈrajt xuˈɮˤaːr ˈwalaħam
gloss_en: I went to the market and bought vegetables and meat.
notes: خُضَار→[xuɮˤaːr] *ḍ voiced lateral emphatic in an everyday food word (watson2011 p.428; asiri2009); past-narrative رُحْت/اشْتَرَيْت; leading و dropped before the ift'aal wasl form to avoid the wāw+waṣl long-mater seam; sun assim السُّوق

sentence: بُكْرَة أَسَافِر أَبْهَا وَأَزُور عَمِّي.
raw: بكرة أسافر أبها وأزور عمي.
ipa: ˈbukra ʔaˈsaːfir ˈʔabhaː waʔaˈzuːr ˈʕammiː
gloss_en: Tomorrow I travel to Abha and visit my uncle.
notes: بُكْرَة + imperfect as future, conservative pan-peninsular; أَبْهَا (ʿAsīr provincial capital) and عَمّ kinship fit the locale (asiri2009); geminate عَمِّي

sentence: اتَّصِل فِيّ اللَّيْلَة بَعْد الْعَشَا.
raw: اتصل في الليلة بعد العشا.
ipa: ˈʔttɑsˤil ˈfijj alˈlajla ˈbaʕd ˈalʕaʃaː
gloss_en: Call me tonight after dinner.
notes: imperative اتَّصِل فِيّ 'call me' with the peninsular فِي government of the verb (asiri2009); geminates اتَّصِل/فِيّ; sun assim اللَّيْلَة

sentence: السَّلَام عَلَيْكُم، كِيف حَالَك؟
raw: السلام عليكم، كيف حالك؟
ipa: assaˈlaːm ʕaˈlajkum ˈkiːf ˈħaːlak
gloss_en: Peace be upon you, how are you?
notes: greeting adjacency pair; السَّلَام عَلَيْكُم + كِيف حَالَك conservative pan-peninsular; sun assim السَّلَام; diphthong عَلَيْكُم; pharyngeals ع/ح; conservative southwestern sedentary phonology (pharyngeals, sun assimilation) per (asiri2009)

sentence: الصَّبْر مِفْتَاح الْفَرَج.
raw: الصبر مفتاح الفرج.
ipa: ɑsˤˈsˤɑbr mifˈtaːħ ˈalfaradʒ
gloss_en: Patience is the key to relief.
notes: conservative pan-Arab proverb suited to a rural closing register; sun assim الصَّبْر; pharyngeal ح; جيم الْفَرَج→[faradʒ]; ǧīm→[dʒ] and southwestern consonant system per (asiri2009)
